source_files: thread-design-elements-v4.html (illustrated component catalogue, must match design-system values)
meta.purpose: Structured, LLM-consumable reference of THREAD 2026 brand/design tokens and usage rules, for generating on-brand copy, components, or reviewing designs for compliance.
meta.usage_notice: Internal use by Filigran comms & marketing. Treat all rules below as constraints when generating or evaluating THREAD 2026 design/content.

brand_palette.name: Coral
brand_palette.role: Tag/badge accent only — never buttons or gradients

brand_palette.name: Pink
brand_palette.role: Tag/badge accent, zone accent (Simulation Zone), speaker category

brand_palette.name: Purple
brand_palette.role: Zone accent (Strategy Room), tag, speaker category

brand_palette.name: Blue
brand_palette.role: UI-safe — buttons, gradient end, zone accent (Signal Room)

brand_palette.name: Cyan
brand_palette.role: Primary interactive accent — links, focus, zone accent (Community Zone)

brand_palette.name: Lime
brand_palette.role: UI-safe — buttons, gradient start, zone accent (Intelligence Lab)

neutral_surface_system.usage: Alternate section background (register band, venue map)
Primary text (any white in the system uses this exact value)
Text-on-light-button color (e.g. text on cyan/lime buttons)

gradient.name: gradient-cool

stops.note: Dedicated gradient mid-stop color, NOT one of the 8 base palette tokens

constraints: This is the ONLY gradient allowed in the system.
Cool colors only — never includes coral, pink, purple, or mauve.
Used for the primary CTA button, hero/section accent lines, and 'The Garden' zone card top-line.

allowed: Primary interactive accent across the whole site (cyan)
Zone accents (Strategy Room = purple, Simulation Zone = pink)
One distinct accent color per zone/speaker category for quick recognition
forbidden: Combining warm colors together into a gradient (avoids an 'Instagram' look)

typefaces_forbidden: any other typeface not listed above

tags_badges.shape: pill, 1.5px border, small colored dot + tinted text/border (never a solid fill)
tags_badges.palette_scope: Full 8-color palette allowed (only component where warm colors + cool colors are both used freely)

zone_cards.description: Each of the 6 experience zones gets one distinct accent, applied only as a 4px top-line + subtle border tint (35% opacity of zone color). Card body stays dark/outlined — never a full color fill.

rules: All content cards (sessions, tickets, speakers, logistics) use --surface background + 1px --border, radius 14–22px.
Emphasis is achieved via border-color change, a colored top-line, or a colored tag inside — never a solid color fill behind text.
Session/keynote cards are never colored solid — outline + tag only.

mosaic_grain.description: The strongest brand signature — echoes 'noise becomes signal'. Always grainy, always scattered (never a solid tiled grid). Used as background texture behind hero sections, register bands, venue maps, and section dividers. Never as a flat color fill.

noise_treatment.contrast_note: ≈ contrast(1.56) applied to the noise layer

mosaic_grain.implementation: CSS grid of 1x1 or occasional 2x2 cells, colors randomized from the 8 palette tokens, each cell overlaid with an SVG fractal-noise filter (feTurbulence, fractalNoise, baseFrequency 0.9, numOctaves 2, stitchTiles).

density_by_context.notes: Radial mask clears the center for text legibility
Higher density, no mask — full block
Dense, used standalone for OG image / social

constraints: Never tile as a perfectly even grid — keep it scattered/organic with occasional clusters.
Never used as a flat color fill substitute.

notes: Blue, Cyan, Lime, Mint on --bg (#0A0A0A) pass AA for large text/UI elements and buttons (dark text on these backgrounds via --ink).

Coral/Pink/Purple/Mauve at full saturation fail AA for small body text on dark background — always used via the tinted-text variants (see tags_badges), never raw hex on raw background for text under 16px.
Primary body copy stays #FBFBFB / muted variant only; color is never the vehicle for conveying body text meaning.

do: Use one distinct accent color per zone/speaker category for quick scanning.
Use grain/mosaic as the recurring signature texture, at Intensity 48 / Contrast 56.
Use only Elms Sans (titles) and IBM Plex Sans / IBM Plex Mono (UI, body, labels).
dont: Don't use coral/pink/purple/mauve in any gradient or button.
Don't fill cards with solid brand color — outline + accent line only.
Don't tile the mosaic as a perfectly even grid — keep it scattered/organic with occasional clusters.
Don't use Inter anywhere in the system.
